zotero.abstractNote: Warmup-Stable-Decay (WSD) has recently emerged as a powerful learning rate schedule for training large language models, enabling efficient continued pre-training and adaptation. Despite its empirical success, the theoretical underpinnings of WSD remain poorly understood. In this work, we provide a comprehensive analysis of WSD, showing that its dynamics are universal and extend well beyond the Transformer architecture. We derive a closed-form solution for the training dynamics of WSD in the context of linear models and show that it effectively acts as a form of implicit regularization. We further validate our findings on a wide range of architectures, including MLPs and CNNs, demonstrating that WSD consistently outperforms standard decay schedules. Our results provide a theoretical justification for the use of WSD and offer practical insights for its application in diverse deep learning tasks.
